concerns.text: FR-001 (Data Aggregation) and SC-004 (Dataset Size) lack explicit task coverage for the NIST and Materials Project fetchers. T012 and T013 are marked [ ] (incomplete) but the plan requires them to be functional to meet the 150-row minimum. Without these implemented, the FR/SC cannot be satisfied.

FR-002 (Preprocessing) and FR-010 (Process Duration) are not fully covered. T016e is marked [ ] (incomplete) and explicitly mandates raising an error if timestamps are missing, whereas FR-002 implies a fallback (imputation) for missing values. The task as written does not implement the required fallback logic, leaving the FR unmet.

FR-008 (Unstructured PSD Data) is partially covered by T014 (flagging) but explicitly excludes the 'configured OCR/extraction fallback' required by US-1 Acceptance Scenario 4 and FR-008. T014b explicitly states 'DO NOT implement OCR', creating a gap where the spec requirement for a fallback is not implemented in the tasks.

FR-003 (Model Training) and FR-009 (Fallback) are not fully covered. T029 (Orchestration) is marked [ ] (incomplete). The fallback logic (catching GPRResourceLimitExceeded and switching to RF) is described in the task description but not implemented. Without T029 being complete, the FR-009 requirement for automatic switching is not met.

FR-006 (Paired t-test) and FR-007 (Power Analysis) are not covered by any implementation task. T027 and T028 are marked [ ] (incomplete). The plan requires these to be implemented to meet the success criteria, but the tasks are not yet actionable.
SC-005 (Computational Feasibility) is not fully covered. T037c (CI time limit) is marked [ ] (incomplete). The spec requires a 6-hour limit, but the task to enforce it in CI is not implemented.

T007b (validate_schema.py) is marked [X] (complete) but the code snippet is truncated ('raise InsufficientD'), indicating the artifact is non-functional. This is a writing/code defect in the task definition, not a coverage gap, but it prevents the task from being valid.

T039 (Fix SC-005 Typo) is a task to fix the spec, not a task to implement a feature. While it addresses a spec flaw, it is not a task that implements a FR/SC. The actual implementation of the 6-hour limit (T037c) is the relevant task for coverage.
